items.q: Which part of the psyche operates on the reality principle?
items.why: Students confuse ego (reality) with superego (morality). The ego mediates between id's demands and reality's constraints. The superego is the internalised moral conscience.
Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.correct: Every member of the target population has an equal chance of selection.
items.misconception: Just grabbing whoever is around (any haphazard method).
items.random_wrong: Picking participants who match the population profile.
items.why: Students treat 'random' as 'casual' or 'haphazard'. True randomness requires a defined sampling frame and a chance procedure (e.g., random number generator).

items.q: Which stage covers ages 0-2 in Piaget's theory?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Sensorimotor. “Preoperational” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Adding a new dog to an existing 'dog' schema is an example of:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Assimilation. “Accommodation” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: At roughly what age does the concrete operational stage begin?
items.why: The real figure is 7. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
